description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, slightly weathered stone surface between them. The block has a substantial, solid appearance, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform height. Subtle discolorations and stains are visible on the stone, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing its geometric simplicity. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment.
description: A rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, solid sides, showing some weathering and discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's length extending away.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooves from the opposite side and some surface stains.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the groove pattern and the block's length.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some surface discoloration and stains are visible, indicating mild weathering from outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
